root_cause: The web service entered a retry storm against the degraded pricing dependency. pricing was slow and failing every request, while web had an 8-worker fixed pool, MAX_RETRIES=4, and a loose 5s dependency timeout. Concurrent order load exceeded the worker count, so every web worker became pinned waiting and retrying pricing calls, causing /healthz to report the pool wedged even though the container was running and CPU was low.
actions_taken: Inspected container status, CPU/memory, web and pricing logs, and host probes for /healthz and /order. Confirmed pricing was degraded and web readiness returned 503 with all workers stalled. Updated only the web service configuration in docker-compose.yaml to set RETRY_STORM_DISABLED=1, then recreated only sreft-web with docker compose up -d web. Verified /healthz returned HTTP 200 and /debug reported bounded mode with max_retries=0, dep_timeout_ms=400, and stalled=0 under continued load. Did not modify pricing, payments, load, or unrelated containers/data.
postmortem: A slow failing dependency triggered retry amplification in web because dependency calls were not tightly bounded and retries held scarce worker slots. The immediate remediation was graceful degradation: cap retries and use a short timeout so order requests fail fast when pricing is unhealthy while web readiness remains available. Follow-ups should make bounded dependency calls the default, add alerts on worker-pool saturation and retry volume, and review retry budgets/backoff so degraded dependencies cannot exhaust serving capacity.
